registry.description: Contentment is the inner state of being satisfied with what one has — not because there is nothing more to desire but because one has come to a settled acceptance of one's present condition. The Greek term (autarkeia) means self-sufficiency in the Stoic sense, but Paul redefines it: not independence from need but the learned ability to be at rest whether in abundance or in want. Contentment is not apathy but a trained inner freedom from the tyranny of circumstances.

terms.medium_def:

1a) at ease (of persons)
1b) quiet (of land)
1c) quiet, ease
1d) ease (subst)

terms.medium_def:
contentment, having all of one’s needs, sufficiency 
a competence of the necessaries of life, sufficiency 2Cor. 9:8; a frame of mind viewing one's lot as sufficient, contentedness, 1Tim. 6:6*
terms.lsj_entry:
αὐτάρκ-εια, ἡ, 
self-sufficiency, independence, [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__II concrete, a sufficiency, [Refs 3rd c.AD+]; a competence, [Refs 2nd c.AD+]
terms.short_def_mounce: contentment, having all of one’s needs, sufficiency
terms.decision_reason: F4: parent G0841 in anchor list, section_type=primary

terms.medium_def:
content; (possibly) self-sufficient 
primarily sufficient or adequate in one's self; contented with one's lot, Phil. 4:11*
terms.lsj_entry:
αὐτάρκ-ης, ες, 
(ἀρκέω) sufficient in oneself, self-supporting, independent of others, ἀνθρώπου σῶμα ἓν οὐδὲν αὔ. ἐστιν[Refs 5th c.BC+]; εἰς εὐδαιμονίαν, of ἀρετή, [Refs 5th c.BC+]helping itself, acting instinctively, [Refs 4th c.BC+]; αὐ. βοή a self-reliant shout, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; οἰκία -έστερον ἑνός, πόλις δ᾽ οἰκίας[Refs 4th c.BC+]; σῶμα αὔ. πρός τι strong enough for a thing, [Refs 5th c.BC+]: with infinitive, able of oneself to do a thing, εἰ γὰρ αὐτάρκη τὰ ψηφίσματα ἦν ἢ ὑμᾶς ἀναγκάζειν κτλ.[Refs 5th c.BC+]. adverb -κως, ἔχειν[Refs 4th c.BC+]: superlative -έστατα, ζῆν[Refs 5th c.BC+]
__II sufficient in quantity, ἀργύριον αὔ. εἰς σιτωνίαν[Refs 1st c.AD+]; ὄξους τὸ -έστατον[Refs 2nd c.AD+]adverb -κως sufficiently, [Refs 1st c.AD+]
